content.question: What is the causal contribution of Hungary's tracking system specifically versus broader socio-economic inequality, residential segregation, and ethnic discrimination in explaining the documented performance gaps and the concentration of Roma students in low-quality vocational tracks?
content.reduction_path: Commission matched-cohort analysis comparing Roma and non-Roma student trajectories through selection gates, controlling for prior achievement, residential location, and family SES; conduct qualitative case studies of transition points (age 10-11 and 12-13) to map explicit selection criteria versus implicit meritocratic screening effects.
